title: Retro-tag event: decisions tag + overlay merge across union reads (D7c)
action: Per CONTEXT D7c: new `decisions tag <id|short8> --tags a,b [--scope s]` appends an append-only event {type:'tag', id, date, target, tags, scope?} to .bee/decisions.jsonl. RED-FIRST: extend test_decisions_propagation.mjs with the new checks FIRST, run red against unmodified code, record via cells verify, then implement. LOCKED APPEND (plan-check B1): the tag append routes through the SAME locked append path dp-3 established — if dp-3 shipped a shared appendDecisionEvent(root, event) primitive, use it; if dp-3 shipped per-writer locking instead, EXTRACT that primitive in this cell and route ALL decision-store writers (log/supersede/redact/tag) through it — the boundary is the primitive, never a name list (critical-patterns 20260710); bare appendJsonl against the decisions store is forbidden from this cell on because archive's temp-write+rename can otherwise eat a concurrent append (lost write). BATCH: `decisions tag --stdin` accepts a JSON array of {target, tags, scope?}; validate EVERY entry first, then append all lines in ONE write under the lock (all-or-nothing — N sequential appends would leave a partial batch on crash). TARGET RESOLUTION: target must resolve to an existing decide/supersede event across the active+archive union (de-dup by id, same reader dp-3 built); unresolvable target → typed refusal naming it, and in batch mode nothing is appended. OVERLAY MERGE (plan-check W2): activeDecisions, filterDecisionEvents AND the --all union path overlay the LATEST tag event's tags/scope (by event date, then file order) onto the target at read time — the overlay map is built from tag events (active file only — tag events are never archived this slice) and applied AFTER union de-dup, so an archived-then-retro-tagged event still reads overlaid. Overlay REPLACES the whole tags array; scope replaced only when the tag event carries one. Tag events are never listed as decisions and never counted by --recent. Mirror to .bee/bin, update command-registry (both mirrors), render + manifest.

truths: a tag event overlays tags/scope onto its target at read time — --tag/--scope filters, active/search, and --all union output all see the overlaid values (archived target included)
latest tag event wins when several target the same decision
batch stdin validates every entry before any write and appends the whole batch in one locked write — an invalid target appends nothing, a crash leaves no partial batch
every decision-store append (log/supersede/redact/tag) routes through the single locked append primitive
legacy events become filterable via retro-tag without any jsonl line being rewritten
RED-FIRST: failing run against unmodified code recorded as red evidence before implementation
prohibitions: Never rewrite or delete existing jsonl lines (append-only integrity)
No tag add/remove semantics — overlay replaces the whole tags array
Tag events are never archived in this slice (they stay in the active file; revisit only if bloat is demonstrated)
No bare appendJsonl against the decisions store from this cell on
